agenda.title: Consider Resolution 25-267, to approve a Lease Agreement for the temporary relocation of Water and Sewer Division Staff to the former Police Department Headquarters Building.
agenda.description: Staff recommends BOMA approve the contract to lease the old Police Department Headquarters building for Spring Hill Water so that staff operations are not disrupted by significant construction at the reclamation plant over the next several years. Dan Allen, ACA, GM Water; Jeremy Vanderford, AGM-Water; Ryan LaMunyon, AGM-Reclamation

agenda.title: Discussion on Resolution to vacate a portion of a drainage easement at 2975 Stewart Campbell Point.
Consider Resolution 25-268, amending Resolution 25-259, temporarily suspending the acceptance and processing of development applications.
agenda.description: Amend resolution 25-259 to expand exemptions to suspension on building permit approvals. Carter Napier, City Administrator; Dan Allen, Assistant City Administrator; and Dara Sanders, Development Services Director

agenda.title: Discussion, Inflow and Infiltration Program Update.
Consider Resolution 25-260, authorizing the acceptance of Offer of Dedication of Road Rights-of-Way and Public Improvements shown on the existing plat for Copper Ridge, Phase 6.
agenda.description: The Planning Commission recommended the acceptance of roads and utility infrastructure for Copper Ridge Phase 6 at the November 10, 2025 meeting. All punch list items have been repaired to City standards and regulations. This resolution accepts the roads and infrastructure in to the City's network. Missy Stahl, CIP Director

agenda.title: Consider Resolution 25-261, authorizing the acceptance of Offer of Dedication of Road Rights-of-Way and Public Improvements shown on the existing plat for Copper Ridge, Phase 8.
agenda.description: The Planning Commission recommended the acceptance of roads and utility infrastructure for Copper Ridge Phase 8 at the November 10, 2025 meeting. All punch list items have been repaired to City standards and regulations. This resolution accepts the roads and infrastructure in to the City's network. Missy Stahl, CIP Director

agenda.title: Consider Resolution 25-262, authorizing the acceptance of Offer of Dedication of Road Rights-of-Way and Public Improvements shown on the existing plat for Summer Meadows, Phase 1 and 2.
agenda.description: The Planning Commission recommended the acceptance of roads and utility infrastructure for Summer Meadows Phase 1 and 2 at the November 10, 2025 meeting.  All punch list items have been repaired to City standards and regulations.  This resolution accepts the roads and infrastructure in to the City's network. Missy Stahl, CIP Director

agenda.title: Consider Resolution 25-263, to adopt a revised Schedule of Authorized Positions in the Public Works Department for Fiscal Year 2026,
agenda.description: The Public Works department desires to establish an in-house construction crew that will be able to assist with preliminary site work on capital improvement projects. Tyler Scroggins, Public Works Director; Chris Clausi, Assistant City Administrator and HR Director

agenda.title: Consider Resolution 25-264, to award bid for Fleet Refueling to Tri-Star Energy, LLC
agenda.description: The city contract for fuel services is due to expire at the end of the year. Staff received one bid from Tri Star on October 29th. Tri Star is the current fuel vendor and staff recommends award of the bid and contract to Tri Star for the Fleet Fueling. Kyle Therriault, Purchasing Agent; Tyler Scroggins, Public Works Director

agenda.title: Consider Resolution 25-265, to amend Resolution 25-224, correcting the General Ledger Code.
agenda.description: The original resolution had an incorrect GL Code, this will correct. David Kerr, GIS Director

agenda.title: Consider Resolution 25-266, authorizing Spring Hill Water Staff to pursue land lease and acquisition opportunities for decentralized water systems.

minutes.title: Call Regular Meeting to Order
minutes.description: 6:05 pm – Mayor Matt Fitterer called the meeting to order.

minutes.title: Stipulation of Members Present
minutes.description: Board of Mayor and Aldermen: Alex Jimenez, Scott Wernert, Erinn Hartwell, Vincent Fuqua, Mayor Matt Fitterer, Vice Mayor Trent Linville, Brent Murray, and Jaimee Davis. Also present, Carter Napier, City Administrator, Patrick Carter, City Attorney, April Goad, City Recorder.

minutes.title: Others Present
minutes.description: Tyler Scroggins, Public Works Director, Rebecca Holden, Finance Director, Don Brite, Police Chief, Lucas Wright, Communications Director, Dara Sanders, Development Services Director, David Kerr, GIS Director,  Jeremy Vanderford, GM Water, and Ryan LaMunyon, GM Reclamation.

minutes.title: Approval of the Agenda
minutes.description: Motion to approve the agenda by Vice Mayor Linville, seconded by Alderman Murray. Motion carried, 8-0 (Unanimous).

minutes.title: Citizen Comments
minutes.description:
Don Alexander, representing developers of June Lake, spoke about road and utility developments around June Lake.

John Maher, local builder, requested that BOMA be slow to consider projects that would utilize the limited sewer capacity.

minutes.title: Mayor's Comments
minutes.description:
Mayor Fitterer shared that the TN Chapter of the American Planning Association has awarded the UGB Associated Interlocal Agreement Planning Project or Tool for a community of greater than 50,000 to Spring Hill.

Mayor Fitterer on behalf of Dan Allen – Seven staff members received certifications through Spring Hill Water, thank you and congratulations.

Mayor Fitterer recognized Spring Hill Water participated in the Duck River Education Day program at Henry Horton State Park.

Mayor Fitterer reminded the BOMA that Friday at 10AM is the ribbon cutting at Fire Station 4.

minutes.title: City Administrator/Department Head Comments
minutes.description:
Mr. Carter Napier, City Administrator, shared that the Community Survey has received over 1200 responses. The survey will conclude on Nov 25.

Ms. Jessica Weaver, Spring Hill Water, shared that the water reclamation facility has reduced nitrogen levels by approximately 55% bringing them below permitting requirements.  She also indicated that Spring Hill was recognized at the Operators Exposition and the TN Nutrient Summit.

minutes.title: Voting Results for Consent Agenda
minutes.description: Motion to approve Consent Agenda by Vice Mayor Linville, seconded by Alderman Murray. Motion carried, 8-0 (Unanimous).

minutes.title: Consider Resolution 25-260, authorizing the acceptance of Offer of Dedication of Road Rights-of-Way and Public Improvements shown on the existing plat for Copper Ridge, Phase 6.
Consider Resolution 25-261, authorizing the acceptance of Offer of Dedication of Road Rights-of-Way and Public Improvements shown on the existing plat for Copper Ridge, Phase 8.
Consider Resolution 25-262, authorizing the acceptance of Offer of Dedication of Road Rights-of-Way and Public Improvements shown on the existing plat for Summer Meadows, Phase 1 and 2.
Consider Resolution 25-263, to adopt a revised Schedule of Authorized Positions in the Public Works Department for Fiscal Year 2026,
Consider Resolution 25-264, to award bid for Fleet Refueling to Tri-Star Energy, LLC
Consider Resolution 25-265, to amend Resolution 25-224, correcting the General Ledger Code.
Consider Resolution 25-266, authorizing Spring Hill Water Staff to pursue land lease and acquisition opportunities for decentralized water systems.
Consider Resolution 25-267, to approve a Lease Agreement for the temporary relocation of Water and Sewer Division Staff to the former Police Department Headquarters Building.
minutes.description:
Alderman Wernert asked questions of Mr. Jeremy Vanderford.

Mr. Napier provided additional information about costs that would be incurred by this relocation.

minutes.title: Consider Resolution 25-268, amending Resolution 25-259, temporarily suspending the acceptance and processing of development applications.
minutes.description:
Mr. Napier shared background and progress.

Ms. Dara Sanders presented two categories of permits that could be released from suspension with the provision that no plumbing is required.

BOMA discussion ensued.

minutes.title: Discussion on Resolution to vacate a portion of a drainage easement at 2975 Stewart Campbell Point.
minutes.description:
• Homeowner identified himself and Ms. Dara Sanders provided an explanation of the homeowner’s request.
  • Ms. Sanders recommended that BOMA vacate their rights to the 10 feet of easement.
  • Discussion ensued.
  • Alderman Linville is in support of vacating the rights to the 10 feet.
  • Request will be moved to the consent agenda for the next meeting.

minutes.title: Discussion, Eastport Farms Follow-up.
minutes.description:
• Greg Gamble of Eastport Farms presented updates and revisions of their master plan for BOMA to consider.
  • Jeff Pape from GBT Realty provided additional information regarding these projects.
  • BOMA members discussed suggestions that were presented.
  • This will not move to the consent agenda for the next meeting

minutes.title: Discussion, Inflow and Infiltration Program Update.
minutes.description:
• Ryan LaMunyon, AGM-Reclamation, provided a background of I&I basic information.
  • Greg Sanford provided an update on the I&I program, approach, and prioritization.
  • BOMA members discussed the I&I program as it currently stands and needs for the future.
  • Staff is directed to bring back a recommendation for the board.

minutes.title: Roundtable Discussion
minutes.description: Alderman Davis thanked Jeremy Vanderford for being in attendance on his birthday.
